channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Why Nairobi keeps flooding and what the city must fix
item.description: 'The day when Nairobi drowns' is an opinion piece that talks about how Nairobi floods again and again whenever it rains heavily, exposing the city’s poor planning and weak drainage systems. The writer...

item.title: Laikipia Police Recover 13 Stolen Cows in Coordinated Security Operation
item.description: In Laikipia County, a joint security operation has successfully recovered thirteen cows that were earlier reported stolen from Segera Location in Laikipia West Sub-County. According to the National Po...

item.title: Global Tensions Rise as US and Iran Conflict Escalates, Israel Strikes Lebanon, and Oil Supplies Released
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the US and Iran has intensified, affecting global oil markets, regional security, and humanitarian conditions. The International Energy Agency (IEA) has announced a histor...

item.title: Assam Police Warns Against Spreading Rumours on LPG and Fuel Shortages
item.description: Guwahati, Assam: Assam Police on Thursday issued a stern warning against spreading false rumours regarding shortages of LPG, diesel, and other petroleum products. The announcement comes a day after th...

item.title: A Practical Guide to Saving with Pag-IBIG MP2 Program
item.description: Pag-IBIG’s MP2 program, or Modified Pag-IBIG II, is a voluntary savings plan ideal for individuals with a conservative risk appetite and a medium-term investment horizon of at least five years. Unlike...

item.title: Pakistan and Muslim countries call for reopening of Al-Aqsa Mosque during Ramazan
item.description: On 12th March 2026, Pakistan along with seven other Muslim countries — Egypt, Jordan, UAE, Indonesia, Turkiye, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar — issued a joint statement strongly condemning Israel’s closure o...

item.title: Borno Area Commander Alleges CP Withholding Allowances and Misusing Police Resources
item.description: The Area Commander of Benisheikh, Borno State, ACP Alex Sani Gwazah, don carry complaint go the Inspector General of Police over how the state Commissioner of Police, CP Naziru Abdulmajid, dey withhol...

item.title: Canada reaches World Baseball Classic quarterfinals for the first time, led by Cal Quantrill
item.description: Canada has achieved a historic milestone by advancing to the quarterfinals of the World Baseball Classic (WBC) for the first time in the tournament's 20-year history. In a decisive game against Cuba i...

item.title: Kerala Conclave: Leaders Discuss State Development Model
item.description: The Kerala Conclave, a significant political and policy event, was scheduled to feature prominent leaders including Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, Shashi Tharoor, Rajeev Chandrasekhar, and John Brit...

item.title: Liza Tarbuck to step down from Radio 2 Saturday evening show
item.description: After 14 years of hosting the Saturday evening slot from 18:00 to 20:00 on BBC Radio 2, Liza Tarbuck has announced she will be stepping down from the show. Tarbuck, who has been a beloved figure on th...

item.title: India Faces Commercial LPG Shortage Amid West Asia Conflict, Domestic Supply Maintained
item.description: India is facing a significant shortage of commercial liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) following the escalation of the conflict in West Asia, impacting hotels, restaurants, and industrial users across the...

item.title: Key Factors That Could Make White-Collar Workers Vulnerable to AI Disruption
item.description: A significant portion of Australia’s workforce may be at risk of job disruption due to advances in artificial intelligence. According to University of Sydney Business School professor Clinton Free, mo...

item.title: Nigerian Military Repels Terrorist Attack on Yobe Base
item.description: Na so the Nigerian military don confirm say some terrorists don attack one military base for Yobe State, but the soldiers wey dey there don repel dem successfully. The attack happen for Goniri, Gujba ...

item.title: Treasury Committee Launches Inquiry into Fairness of Student Loan Repayment Terms
item.description: The Treasury Committee in the UK Parliament has announced an inquiry into the student loans system amid growing concerns over repayment terms and conditions. This follows widespread dissatisfaction am...

item.title: Kenya and Rwanda Agree on Fintech Passporting to Ease Cross-Border Payments
item.description: Kenya and Rwanda have taken a significant step towards boosting regional financial integration by signing the Kigali Declaration on Fintech License Passporting. This agreement, inked by the National B...

item.title: UK Regulators Demand Stronger Online Protections for Children from Major Tech Platforms
item.description: Following a parliamentary vote rejecting a blanket ban on social media for under-16s, UK regulators Ofcom and the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) have intensified pressure on major tech platfo...

item.title: Kathie Lee Gifford Expresses Frustration with Length of LGBTQ Acronym While Affirming Love and Non-Judgment in Faith-Based Interview
item.description: In a recent interview with conservative commentator Tomi Lahren on her Fox Nation show, retired television host Kathie Lee Gifford discussed balancing her Christian faith with acceptance of the LGBTQ ...

item.title: Iranian Women Footballers Granted Asylum in Australia Following Escape from Team Officials
item.description: Five Iranian women footballers have been granted asylum in Australia after escaping strict supervision by Iranian government minders during the Asian Cup. The team, including captain Zahra Ghanbari, f...

item.title: Prime Minister Shehbaz forwards Nehal Hashmi’s name for Sindh Governor post
item.description: In an unexpected development, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has put forward PML-N leader Nehal Hashmi for the position of Sindh Governor. The announcement came via a statement from the Prime Minister’...

item.title: Protest statue of Trump and Epstein installed on National Mall
item.description: A provocative new statue depicting former US President Donald Trump alongside the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein was installed on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. The sculpture, enti...
